requirements.description: User can view all notes in a hierarchical list
Notes are displayed with their titles visible
User can see a sidebar or navigation to browse notes
User can access a search function
Search input field is visible or accessible
User can access an AI chat or assistant feature
Chat interface has an input field for questions
User can see a button or option to create a new note
New note creation is accessible from the main screen
Notes appear to support nesting or hierarchy
Indentation or folder structure is visible for nested notes
Editor area is visible when viewing/editing a note
Formatting toolbar or options are available
User can see block-level content (paragraphs, headings, lists)
App has mobile-appropriate navigation (bottom tabs or hamburger menu)
Touch targets appear appropriately sized for mobile
Content is readable without horizontal scrolling

requirement.description: User can view all notes in a hierarchical list

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a list of notes, but it is not clear if the notes are in a hierarchical format or organized based on categories. The notes appear to be simple text entries without any visible sub-categories or indentation. Additional information or a different view within the app would be needed to fully determine if the requirement has been met.
results.notes: The screenshot does not provide enough context to judge whether the notes are categorized or organized in a hierarchical manner. It is essential to verify that the notes can be expanded or collapsed to show sub-categories and indentation if such features exist.

requirement.description: Notes are displayed with their titles visible

results.evidence: The image does not show any notes displayed with their titles visible.
results.notes: There are some elements that appear to be notes within a list, but it is unclear whether these are individual notes or part of another structure. The lack of clear note titles makes it difficult to determine if the requirement is met.

requirement.description: User can see a sidebar or navigation to browse notes

results.evidence: No sidebar or navigation visible in the screenshot.
results.notes: The user appears to be viewing a single note without any indication of a navigation bar or sidebar. This does not meet the requirement of a 'sidebar or navigation' to browse notes.

requirement.description: User can access a search function

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a visible search function within the app, as indicated by the presence of a magnifying glass icon in the top right corner, which is commonly associated with search functions.
results.notes: There is no indication in this screenshot that additional functionalities of the search function are present or accessible. The image only depicts a single aspect of the search functionality.

requirement.description: Search input field is visible or accessible

results.evidence: No search input field is visible in the screenshot.
results.notes: Untested if the search functionality works when activated.

requirement.description: User can access an AI chat or assistant feature

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a chatbot interface within an app, indicating that users can access an AI chat or assistant feature.
results.notes: Note: The chatbot interface appears to be functional and accessible. It may have limited functionality based on the content displayed in this snapshot.

requirement.description: Chat interface has an input field for questions

results.evidence: The image does not show any input field for questions within the chat interface.
results.notes: The chat interface appears to lack an input field for asking questions.

requirement.description: User can see a button or option to create a new note

results.evidence: In the provided screenshot, there is no visible button or option for creating a new note.
results.notes: The user interface shows a search bar instead of a button for creating a new note.

requirement.description: New note creation is accessible from the main screen

results.evidence: The image does not show a main screen or any way to create notes.
results.notes: It is unclear if the app has a main screen from this screenshot. There are no visible buttons or options that relate to creating notes.

requirement.description: Notes appear to support nesting or hierarchy

results.evidence: There is a single note with text content and a sub-note directly below it, but there does not appear to be any further level of nesting or hierarchy within the visible area.
results.notes: It's possible that there are additional notes not visible in this screenshot. The requirement seems to imply a multi-level nesting structure, which is not evident from the provided image.

requirement.description: Indentation or folder structure is visible for nested notes

results.evidence: The screenshot does not provide information about indentation or folder structure for nested notes.
results.notes: From this image, I cannot determine if the requirement 'Indentation or folder structure is visible for nested notes' is met. To make an accurate assessment of the requirement, I would need additional context and information beyond what the screenshot provides.

requirement.description: Editor area is visible when viewing/editing a note

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a mobile phone screen with an Instagram app interface. The requirement 'Editor area is visible when viewing/editing a note' pertains to the block editor functionality in the app. In this image, there is no evidence of the block editor being present or accessible. The UI does not display any visible options to access the block editor. Hence, it can be concluded that the block editor is not available for use on this screen.
results.notes: It's unclear whether this specific requirement refers to viewing a note or editing one since there are no visual clues for either action. The image does not provide enough information to determine if the app fulfills other aspects of the requirement in the context of a block editor.

requirement.description: Formatting toolbar or options are available

results.evidence: The image does not show a formatting toolbar or options available within the block editor on the mobile app.
results.notes: Untested screenshot of an iOS app that does not meet requirement 'Formatting toolbar or options are available' in Block Editor category with high priority. No formatting options are visible, which is a failure for this requirement.

requirement.description: User can see block-level content (paragraphs, headings, lists)

results.evidence: The screenshot displays a paragraph of text within the block editor, which is part of the user interface that suggests this requirement is met.
results.notes: However, there are no visible headings or lists in the provided screenshot. The 'block-level content' requirement is partially met as it only shows a single paragraph.

requirement.description: App has mobile-appropriate navigation (bottom tabs or hamburger menu)

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a mobile device displaying a social media app with a bottom navigation bar that includes three visible tabs: Home, Page not found, and Camera. This indicates that the app has mobile-appropriate navigation.
results.notes: It is important to note that it's possible that more navigation options are available when navigating through different states of the app (e.g., while browsing a profile or when searching for content). Therefore, this assessment is based only on the immediate visible state of the app in the screenshot and does not cover all possible navigation scenarios.

requirement.description: Touch targets appear appropriately sized for mobile

results.evidence: The app's touch targets appear appropriately sized for mobile in general, but there is a small touch target at the bottom of the screen with a circular icon that may not be easily tappable on all devices due to its size.
results.notes: It would be beneficial to test this on different devices and under various conditions (e.g., zoomed-in viewports) to ensure accessibility.

requirement.description: Content is readable without horizontal scrolling

results.evidence: The content on the screen is clearly readable without horizontal scrolling.
results.notes: The image shows a screenshot of an iPhone displaying a collection view with various app icons, and the content is easily legible without any need to scroll horizontally.
